devanagari: कुरुरादि सप्तगुरूणां वर्णनम् अवधूतोपाख्यानसमाप्तिश्च । श्रीब्राह्मण उवाच । परिग्रहो हि दुःखाय यद् यत् प्रियतमं नृणाम् । अनन्तं सुखमाप्नो...
english_devnagari: kururādi saptagurūṇāṃ varṇanam avadhūtopākhyānasamāptiścaśrībrāhmaṇa uvācaparigraho hi duḥkhāya yad yat priyatamaṃ nṛṇāmanantaṃ sukhamāpnoti tad vidvān yastvakiñcanaḥ
translation:
The Brāhmaṇa continued:
Acquisition of whatever persons crave most to possess is certainly the cause of misery. He who realizes this and (wishing to overcome his propensity for possession) becomes a complete ‘Have-not’, attains infinite happiness.

devanagari: सामिषं कुररं जघ्नुः बलिनो ये निरामिषाः । तदामिषं परित्यज्य स सुखं समविन्दत ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sāmiṣaṃ kuraraṃ jaghnuḥ balino ye nirāmiṣāḥtadāmiṣaṃ parityajya sa sukhaṃ samavindata
translation: More powerful birds of prey which had got no piece of flesh, attacked and pecked the he-osprey who possessed one. But by dropping that flesh, it (the he-osprey) got relief and happiness.

devanagari: न मे मानापमानौ स्तो न चिन्ता गेहपुत्रिणाम् । आत्मक्रीड आत्मरतिः विचरामीह बालवत् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: na me mānāpamānau sto na cintā gehaputriṇāmātmakrīḍa ātmaratiḥ vicarāmīha bālavat
translation: I am not affected by honour or dishonour. I have no anxiety for my house, wife and children (as I have none). Like a child, I range over the world at will, sporting in the bliss of Ātman and delighted within my own Soul.

devanagari: द्वावेव चिन्तया मुक्तौ परमानन्द आप्लुतौ । यो विमुग्धो जडो बालो यो गुणेभ्यः परं गतः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: dvāveva cintayā muktau paramānanda āplutauyo vimugdho jaḍo bālo yo guṇebhyaḥ paraṃ gataḥ
translation: (O king, in this world) only two (types of) persons are free from anxiety and are immersed in the Supreme bliss—one is an innocent ignorant child and the other is one who has transcended the guṇas.

devanagari: क्वचित् कुमारी त्वात्मानं वृणानान् गृहमागतान् । स्वयं तान् अर्हयामास क्वापि यातेषु बन्धुषु ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacit kumārī tvātmānaṃ vṛṇānān gṛhamāgatānsvayaṃ tān arhayāmāsa kvāpi yāteṣu bandhuṣu
translation: In a certain place, a maiden herself had to receive the guests who had come to see her (for selecting her as a bride) while her relatives (parents etc.) had gone out somewhere.

devanagari: तेषम् अभ्यवहारार्थं शालीन् रहसि पार्थिव । अवघ्नन्त्याः प्रकोष्ठस्थाः चक्रुः शङ्खाः स्वनं महत् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣam abhyavahārārthaṃ śālīn rahasi pārthivaavaghnantyāḥ prakoṣṭhasthāḥ cakruḥ śaṅkhāḥ svanaṃ mahat
translation: While she was pounding alone (secretly) paddy for entertaining them with food, the bangles of shell in her wrists began to give out loud jingling sound.

devanagari: सा तत् जुगुप्सितं मत्वा महती व्रीडिता ततः । बभञ्जैकैकशः शङ्खान् द्वौ द्वौ पाण्योरशेषयत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sā tat jugupsitaṃ matvā mahatī vrīḍitā tataḥbabhañjaikaikaśaḥ śaṅkhān dvau dvau pāṇyoraśeṣayat
translation: The intelligent girl felt it shameful (to disclose that the bride had to do such work), broke the bangles one by one till only two remained on each of her wrists.

devanagari: उभयोरप्यभूद् घोषो ह्यवघ्नन्त्याः स्म शंखयोः । तत्राप्येकं निरभिदद् एकस्मात् नाभवद् ध्वनिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ubhayorapyabhūd ghoṣo hyavaghnantyāḥ sma śaṃkhayoḥtatrāpyekaṃ nirabhidad ekasmāt nābhavad dhvaniḥ
translation: Even the pair of bangles created a noise as she pounded (the paddy). She, therefore broke one from each pair (with the result that) no sound was produced from the remaining one.

devanagari: अन्वशिक्षमिमं तस्या उपदेशमरिन्दम । लोकान् अनुचरन् एतान् लोकतत्त्वविवित्सया ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: anvaśikṣamimaṃ tasyā upadeśamarindamalokān anucaran etān lokatattvavivitsayā
translation: O vanquisher of enemies, I learnt this lesson from her, while I was roaming over the world with the desire of knowing the true nature of the world.

devanagari: वासे बहूनां कलहो भवेत् वार्ता द्वयोरपि । एक एव चरेत् तस्मात् कुमार्या इव कङ्कणः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: vāse bahūnāṃ kalaho bhavet vārtā dvayorapieka eva caret tasmāt kumāryā iva kaṅkaṇaḥ
translation: If many persons live together there will be quarrels; if two live together there is a talk. Hence a person should wander alone without any companion like the single bangle on the wrist of the girl.

devanagari: मन एकत्र संयुञ्ज्यात् जितश्वासो जितासनः । वैराग्याभ्यासयोगेन ध्रियमाणमतन्द्रितः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: mana ekatra saṃyuñjyāt jitaśvāso jitāsanaḥvairāgyābhyāsayogena dhriyamāṇamatandritaḥ
translation: Having achieved firmness of the sitting posture and controlled the breath, a person should concentrate his mind (like an archer on his target and by means of renunciation and Yogic practice, he should vigilantly keep his mind steady.

devanagari: यस्मिन्मनो लब्धपदं यदेतत् । शनैः शनैः मुञ्चति कर्मरेणून् । सत्त्वेन वृद्धेन रजस्तमश्च । विधूय निर्वाणमुपैत्यनिन्धनम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yasminmano labdhapadaṃ yadetatśanaiḥ śanaiḥ muñcati karmareṇūnsattvena vṛddhena rajastamaścavidhūya nirvāṇamupaityanindhanam
translation: The mind, when concentrated in him, sheds off gradually the particles of karma-dust. With the development of Sattva guṇa, he shakes off the Rājasic and Tāmasic tendencies and enters into Nirvāṇa, like a fire (automatically) extinguished (when the fuel is completely burnt). (Such a stage is known as asamprajñāta samādhi).

devanagari: तदैवमात्मन्यवरुद्धचित्तो । न वेद किञ्चिद् बहिरन्तरं वा । यथेषुकारो नृपतिं व्रजन्तं । इषौ गतात्मा न ददर्श पार्श्वे ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tadaivamātmanyavaruddhacittona veda kiñcid bahirantaraṃ vāyatheṣukāro nṛpatiṃ vrajantaṃiṣau gatātmā na dadarśa pārśve
translation: In that stage, a person whose mind is completely absorbed in the meditation of his Self, does not remain conscious of anything inside, or outside just as the manufacturer of shafts was so much absorbed in his work of making arrows that he was not aware of the procession of the king passing by his smithy.

devanagari: एकचार्यनिकेतः स्याद् अप्रमत्तो गुहाशयः । अलक्ष्यमाण आचारैः मुनिरेकोऽल्पभाषणः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ekacāryaniketaḥ syād apramatto guhāśayaḥalakṣyamāṇa ācāraiḥ munireko'lpabhāṣaṇaḥ
translation: Like a serpent a sage, therefore, should go about alone, (avoiding company of others). have no house, should always be vigilant (in his spiritual practice) and take shelter in caves[1]. He should see to it that he is not marked out by his behaviour (just as the poisonousness or non-poisonousness of a serpent is not discernible from his trail). He should be sparing of words and lead a solitary life.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī gives better interpretation: ‘He should thoroughly and uninterruptedly be absorbed in the meditation of the Lord abiding in the cave of his heart’.

devanagari: गृहारम्भोऽतिदुःखाय विफलश्चाध्रुवात्मनः । सर्पः परकृतं वेश्म प्रविश्य सुखमेधते ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhārambho'tiduḥkhāya viphalaścādhruvātmanaḥsarpaḥ parakṛtaṃ veśma praviśya sukhamedhate
translation: In the case of man who himself is mortal, the attempt to build a house (and maintenance of a family, etc. implied in it) is fraught with great troubles and is fruitless ultimately. But a serpent enters into a house prepared by others (such as an ant-hill or a hole prepared by a mouse) and lives happily. A sage should emulate his example and not bother about constructing a residence.

devanagari: एको नारायणो देवः पूर्वसृष्टं स्वमायया । संहृत्य कालकलया कल्पान्त इदमीश्वरः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: eko nārāyaṇo devaḥ pūrvasṛṣṭaṃ svamāyayāsaṃhṛtya kālakalayā kalpānta idamīśvaraḥ
translation: Like a spider withdrawing its web spun out and spread over by it, the Almighty Lord Nārāyaṇa through his potency of time, withdraws, at the end of the Kalpa, this universe which he has created through his Māyā Potency.

devanagari: एक एवाद्वितीयोऽभूत् आत्माधारोऽखिलाश्रयः । कालेनात्मानुभावेन साम्यं नीतासु शक्तिषु । सत्त्वादिष्वादिपुरुषः प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: eka evādvitīyo'bhūt ātmādhāro'khilāśrayaḥkālenātmānubhāvena sāmyaṃ nītāsu śaktiṣusattvādiṣvādipuruṣaḥ pradhānapuruṣeśvaraḥ
translation: The Supreme Lord (then) remains alone, one without a second (i.e. devoid of any differentiation) himself self- supported (self-born). He was the support of all the universe. When he has reduced all his powers like sattva, rajas and tamas to the stale of equilibrium with his own force called time, he exists as the First (Ancient-most, unborn) Person, the controller of Pradhāna (wherein, all the Guṇas are merged) and of Puruṣa (that is of Matter and Spirit).

devanagari: परावराणां परम आस्ते कैवल्यसंज्ञितः । केवलानुभवानन्द सन्दोहो निरुपाधिकः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: parāvarāṇāṃ parama āste kaivalyasaṃjñitaḥkevalānubhavānanda sandoho nirupādhikaḥ
translation: He is the highest goal to be attained by god Brahmā and other liberated Souls. He is designated (by Śrutis) as Kevala (Absolute, unconditioned). He is (as it were) a vast reservoir of absolute bliss unconditioned by any limitations.

devanagari: केवलात्मानुभावेन स्वमायां त्रिगुणात्मिकाम् । सङ्क्षोभयन् सृजत्यादौ तया सूत्रमरिन्दम ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kevalātmānubhāvena svamāyāṃ triguṇātmikāmsaṅkṣobhayan sṛjatyādau tayā sūtramarindama
translation: [2] By his Potency (called time) which needs no other assistance, he agitated his Māyā constituted of three guṇas and (through this agitated Māyā) created the Sūtra (thread) or the principle called Mahat, O vanquisher of enemies (like lust, anger, etc. Hence, you need not be afraid of the power of this Sūtra or Mahat).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī: He is not conditioned by a gross, physical body. He is the Antaryāmin (Inner controller) of Kāla (Time). At his own free will, he manifests himself through Time and agitates Māyā i.e. Prakṛti which is under his control and makes her prone to creation. He creates through Prakṛti, the thread (Sūtra) or (Prāṇa) and god Brahmā.

devanagari: तामाहुः त्रिगुणव्यक्तिं सृजन्तीं विश्वतोमुखम् । यस्मिन् प्रोतम् इदं विश्वं येन संसरते पुमान् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tāmāhuḥ triguṇavyaktiṃ sṛjantīṃ viśvatomukhamyasmin protam idaṃ viśvaṃ yena saṃsarate pumān
translation: [3] They have declared the Sūtra to be the manifestation of three guṇas which evolves this universe of multifarious aspects. It is in the Sūtra that the universe is woven. It is due to Sūtra (in the form of vital breath that a person wanders through the cycle of births and deaths[4].
purport:
Footnote [3]:
They saw that the three guṇas are evolved out of Māyā. It is in the Māyā or Prakṛti that the universe is woven and Jīva gets involved in Saṃsāra as it is invested with Prakṛti—Padaratnāvalī

Footnote [4]:
Cf. Vāyur vai gautama sūtram, vāyunā vai sūtreṇā'yaṃ ca lokaḥ, paraś ca lokaḥ sarvāṇi ca bhūtāni sandṛbdhāni /—Bṛhad. Up. 3.7.2

devanagari: यथोर्णनाभिः हृदयाद् ऊर्णां सन्तत्य वक्त्रतः । तया विहृत्य भूयस्तां ग्रसत्येवं महेश्वरः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yathorṇanābhiḥ hṛdayād ūrṇāṃ santatya vaktrataḥtayā vihṛtya bhūyastāṃ grasatyevaṃ maheśvaraḥ
translation: Just as a spider extends through its mouth the cobweb from its heart, sports with it and again swallows it, so does the Supreme Ruler evolve, protect and withdraw the universe.

devanagari: यत्र यत्र मनो देही धारयेत् सकलं धिया । स्नेहाद् द्वेषाद् भयाद् वापि याति तत् तत्स्वरूपताम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra yatra mano dehī dhārayet sakalaṃ dhiyāsnehād dveṣād bhayād vāpi yāti tat tatsvarūpatām
translation: It may be out of love, hatred or fear, but on whatever object an embodied being concentrates his mind entirely, he attains to the semblance of that object.

devanagari: कीटः पेशस्कृतं ध्यायन् कुड्यां तेन प्रवेशितः । याति तत्सात्मतां राजन् पूर्वरूपमसन्त्यजन् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kīṭaḥ peśaskṛtaṃ dhyāyan kuḍyāṃ tena praveśitaḥyāti tatsātmatāṃ rājan pūrvarūpamasantyajan
translation: The larva (captured and) confined in its (captor’s) nest by the wasp, O King, continuously broods over it (through fear), and without losing its original body, it assumes (i.e. is transformed into) the semblance of the wasp.

devanagari: एवं गुरुभ्य एतेभ्य एषा मे शिक्षिता मतिः । स्वात्मोपशिक्षितां बुद्धिं श्रृणु मे वदतः प्रभो ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ gurubhya etebhya eṣā me śikṣitā matiḥsvātmopaśikṣitāṃ buddhiṃ śrṛṇu me vadataḥ prabho
translation: In this manner, from these several preceptors I learnt this wisdom. Now listen as I shall explain to you the wisdom I learnt from my body, O king.

devanagari: देहो गुरुर्मम विरक्तिविवेकहेतुः । बिभ्रत् स्म सत्त्वनिधनं सततार्त्युदर्कम् । तत्त्वान्यनेन विमृशामि यथा तथापि । पारक्यमित्यवसितो विचराम...
english_devnagari: deho gururmama viraktivivekahetuḥbibhrat sma sattvanidhanaṃ satatārtyudarkamtattvānyanena vimṛśāmi yathā tathāpipārakyamityavasito vicarāmyasaṅgaḥ
translation: My body has been my preceptor as it causes in me dispassion and (engenders in me) discrimination. It creates dispassion as it is subject to birth and death and is in the (final analysis) the source of ever-increasing afflictions. It promotes discrimination, as through it I comprehend correctly the fundamental principles of this universe. I, however, have arrived at the conclusion that this body belongs to others (parents who gave birth to it—or birds and beasts of prey who may eat it). Realizing this, I roam about without entertaining any attachment to the body.

devanagari: जायात्मजार्थपशुभृत्यगृहाप्तवर्गान् । पुष्णाति यत्प्रियचिकीर्षुतया वितन्वन् । स्वान्ते सकृच्छ्रमवरुद्धधनः स देहः । सृष्ट्वास्य बीजमवसीदत...
english_devnagari: jāyātmajārthapaśubhṛtyagṛhāptavargānpuṣṇāti yatpriyacikīrṣutayā vitanvansvānte sakṛcchramavaruddhadhanaḥ sa dehaḥsṛṣṭvāsya bījamavasīdati vṛkṣadharmā
translation: It is with the desire of securing pleasure and comforts for this body that a person, with great strain to himself maintains his wife, children, earns money, raises cattle, and keeps servants, houses and relatives. But in the end (at the time of death), like a tree leaving its seed behind for the growth of another tree, it perishes providing for (his re-birth in) another body.

devanagari: जिह्वैकतोऽमुमपकर्षति कर्हि तर्षा । शिश्नोऽन्यतस्त्वगुदरं श्रवणं कुतश्चित् । घ्राणोऽन्यतश्चपलदृक् क्व च कर्मशक्तिः । बह्व्यः सपत्‍न्य इव...
english_devnagari: jihvaikato'mumapakarṣati karhi tarṣāśiśno'nyatastvagudaraṃ śravaṇaṃ kutaścitghrāṇo'nyataścapaladṛk kva ca karmaśaktiḥbahvyaḥ sapat‍nya iva gehapatiṃ lunanti
translation: The tongue drags a person in one direction to satisfy his taste with tasty eatables; thirst, to another; the organ of generation, still to another direction for sexual intercourse; the senses of touch, hearing and stomach severally to different directions; the nose (sense of smell) still to a different direction (to enjoy perfumes); his quick eyesight, to another and all his organs of action in their own respective directions. In this way, like co-wives pulling (lit. tearing up) the lord of their house (in their own direction), the senses and organs tear up a person.

devanagari: सृष्ट्वा पुराणि विविधान्यजयाऽत्मशक्त्या । वृक्षान् सरीसृप पशून् खगदंशमत्स्यान् । तैस्तैरतुष्टहृदयः पुरुषं विधाय । ब्रह्मावलोकधिषणं मुदम...
english_devnagari: sṛṣṭvā purāṇi vividhānyajayā'tmaśaktyāvṛkṣān sarīsṛpa paśūn khagadaṃśamatsyāntaistairatuṣṭahṛdayaḥ puruṣaṃ vidhāyabrahmāvalokadhiṣaṇaṃ mudamāpa devaḥ
translation: Having created, with his inherent power, the birthless Māyā, various kinds of bodies (i.e. species) such as those of plants and trees, reptiles, mammals, birds, biting insects (like mosquitos), fish (and other acquatic animals), he remained unsatisfied. But when God created the human being with an intellectual capacity capable of realizing the Brahman or Supreme Being, He became delighted.[5]

devanagari: लब्ध्वा सुदुर्लभमिदं बहुसंभवान्ते । मानुष्यमर्थदमनित्यमपीह धीरः । तूर्णं यतेत न पतेदनुमृत्यु यावन् । निःश्रेयसाय विषयः खलु सर्वतः स्यात...
english_devnagari: labdhvā sudurlabhamidaṃ bahusaṃbhavāntemānuṣyamarthadamanityamapīha dhīraḥtūrṇaṃ yateta na patedanumṛtyu yāvanniḥśreyasāya viṣayaḥ khalu sarvataḥ syāt
translation: Having secured, at the end of many transmigrations, birth in this very rare human body which, though transient, is capable of attaining the highest object in life, viz. Mokṣa (liberation), a wise person should endeavour at the earliest, to attain the summum bonum, before his body had not fallen a prey to death. For, objects of senses can, however, be enjoyed everywhere in all species.

devanagari: एवं सञ्जातवैराग्यो विज्ञानालोक आत्मनि । विचरामि महीमेतां मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहङ्कृतिः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sañjātavairāgyo vijñānāloka ātmanivicarāmi mahīmetāṃ muktasaṅgo'nahaṅkṛtiḥ
translation: In this manner, with the sense of dispassion developed and with the light of spiritual wisdom dawned in me, I wander over the earth, free from all attachments and egotism.

devanagari: न ह्येकस्मात् गुरोर्ज्ञानं सुस्थिरं स्यात् सुपुष्कलम् । ब्रह्मैतदद्वितीयं वै गीयते बहुधर्षिभिः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyekasmāt gurorjñānaṃ susthiraṃ syāt supuṣkalambrahmaitadadvitīyaṃ vai gīyate bahudharṣibhiḥ
translation: Knowledge obtained from one preceptor may not be stable, convincing and comprehensive[6]. This Brahman is verily one without a second, but it is sung variously by different sages.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Here Bhāvāratha Dīpikā clarifies that Upaniṣadic sages like Śvetaketu and Bhṛgu did not have more than one spiritual preceptor. But the spiritual guidance received by them pertained to the identity of Brahman & the Ātman. Here the ‘knowledge’ means clarifications of minor doubts which can be resolved by logical reasoning; for this a number of ‘preceptors’ or ‘examples’ are useful.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । इत्युक्त्वा स यदुं विप्रः तमामन्त्र्य गभीरधीः । वन्दितोऽभ्यर्थितो राज्ञा ययौ प्रीतो यथागतम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācaityuktvā sa yaduṃ vipraḥ tamāmantrya gabhīradhīḥvandito'bhyarthito rājñā yayau prīto yathāgatam
translation:
The Lord said:
In this manner, the Brāhmaṇa, of deep knowledge, replied all the questions of Yadu and took his leave of Yadu. He was duly respected and prayed by the king. With pleasure, the Brāhmaṇa wended his way.

devanagari: अवधूतवचः श्रुत्वा पूर्वेषां नः स पूर्वजः । सर्वसङ्गविनिर्मुक्तः समचित्तो बभूव ह ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: avadhūtavacaḥ śrutvā pūrveṣāṃ naḥ sa pūrvajaḥsarvasaṅgavinirmuktaḥ samacitto babhūva ha
translation:
Having carefully listened to the philosophical exposition of the Avadhūta, Yadu, the ancestor of our ancestors, became completely free from all types of attachment and looked upon all as equals.
[back to top]
